clues.title: Gold Rush Ground
clues.theme: Gold Rush landfill — the land beneath Pier 39 was created from Gold Rush-era ship debris

clues.title: After the Fires
clues.theme: 1906 earthquake rebuild — find evidence of post-disaster reconstruction near the waterfront

clues.title: Federal Concrete
clues.theme: WPA construction at Aquatic Park — the bathhouse and bleachers were built by New Deal labor

clues.title: A Woman With a Ladder
clues.theme: Lillie Coit — her connection to SF volunteer firefighters is memorialized near Aquatic Park

clues.title: Before Bridges
clues.theme: Pre-bridge rowing history at Aquatic Park — the bay was the highway before the Golden Gate opened

clues.title: Strategy in Steel
clues.theme: WWII Liberty ships at Hyde Street Pier — mass-produced cargo ships that supplied the Allied war effort

clues.title: Silent Pacific
clues.theme: USS Pampanito submarine at Hyde Street Pier — Fleet submarine that sank 6 Japanese ships

clues.title: The Occupation
clues.theme: 1969 Native American occupation of Alcatraz — 89 activists held the island for 19 months demanding treaty rights

clues.title: The Strike That Shook the Port
clues.theme: 1934 longshoremen's strike at Pier 23 — a general strike shut down SF for 4 days and changed American labor law

clues.title: Empire of Goods
clues.theme: Port of SF industrial history at Pier 7 — once the busiest port on the Pacific Coast

clues.title: The Bridge That Changed Everything
clues.theme: Bay Bridge at Rincon Park — opened 1936, six months before the Golden Gate, at twice the cost

clues.title: Before the Bridge
clues.theme: Ferry Building — before 1937, 50,000 commuters a day crossed the bay by ferry; the Bridge killed the system overnight

clues.title: The Fountain They Hated
clues.theme: Vaillancourt Fountain at Justin Herman Plaza — installed 1971, condemned as an eyesore by critics, beloved by skaters

clues.title: The Murals They Tried to Hide
clues.theme: Censored WPA murals at Rincon Center — 1941 labor imagery so charged the post office locked the building for months

clues.title: The Pyramid That Wasn't Welcome
clues.theme: Transamerica Pyramid — completed 1972; protestors called it an alien intrusion, now it's SF's most recognized silhouette

clues.title: The Trial of the Poem
clues.theme: City Lights Bookstore — Allen Ginsberg's Howl was seized here in 1957; the obscenity trial made Beat literature famous

clues.title: The Bar Jack Built
clues.theme: Vesuvio Cafe — Jack Kerouac stood up Neal Cassady to drink here the night he was supposed to meet Henry Miller

clues.title: The Saints and the Immigrants
clues.theme: Washington Square Park — heart of Italian North Beach; the volunteer fire brigade statue honors the community that rebuilt SF

clues.title: The Marriage That Made Headlines
clues.theme: Saints Peter & Paul Church — DiMaggio and Monroe posed here for photos after their 1954 City Hall wedding

clues.title: The Tower She Inspired
clues.theme: Coit Tower exterior — built with Lillie Coit's bequest; she left one-third of her estate to beautify SF

clues.title: The Art They Tried to Lock Away
clues.theme: Coit Tower murals — 1934 WPA frescoes included hammer-and-sickle imagery; the tower was padlocked before opening

clues.title: The Fishermen's Trade
clues.theme: Pier 45 — Italian immigrant fishing families built San Francisco's Dungeness crab industry from scratch in the 1880s

clues.title: The Last D-Day Ship
clues.theme: SS Jeremiah O'Brien at Pier 45 — one of only two surviving Liberty ships that actually participated in the D-Day invasion

clues.title: The Factory That Became a Destination
clues.theme: Ghirardelli Square — converted in 1964, one of America's first adaptive reuse projects; the factory clock tower was preserved

clues.title: The Game They Brought With Them
clues.theme: Victorian Park bocce courts — Italian immigrants built permanent bocce courts here in the early 1900s; still active today

clues.title: The Port of Departure
clues.theme: Fort Mason Great Meadow — 1.6 million American soldiers shipped out from these piers in WWII, more than any other port

clues.title: When Art Replaced Ammo
clues.theme: Fort Mason Center Building B — decommissioned 1972, became one of the first counterculture arts centers in America

clues.title: The Oldest Defense
clues.theme: Black Point Battery at Fort Mason — Civil War artillery fortification built 1863, the oldest surviving military site on this route

clues.title: Waymo Transition
clues.theme: Call a Waymo from your current location and ride to 1083 Lombard Street. Screenshot your completed ride.

clues.title: Chosen Inconvenience
clues.theme: Lombard Street — residents voted to have it made crooked in 1922 to slow runaway cars on the steep grade

clues.title: Democracy Saved It
clues.theme: Powell-Hyde cable car — a 1947 citizen vote stopped the mayor from scrapping the entire cable car system

clues.title: Improvisation and Geography
clues.theme: Grateful Dead — formed in the Haight in 1965; their free outdoor concerts reshaped what a rock show could be

clues.title: Succession and Shock
clues.theme: Dianne Feinstein — discovered Harvey Milk's body and became mayor that day; served 10 years defining modern SF politics

clues.title: Loma Prieta Synthesis
clues.theme: 1989 earthquake — the 15-second quake during the World Series killed 63 people and collapsed the Embarcadero Freeway, accidentally beautifying the waterfront
